items.label: XS
items.prompt: [IMPORTANT: Keep your response under 20 words. Be extremely brief.]

items.label: S
items.prompt: [IMPORTANT: Keep your response under 100 words. Be concise.]

items.label: M
items.prompt: [IMPORTANT: Keep your response under 300 words. Be moderately detailed.]

items.label: XL
items.prompt: [Provide the most comprehensive and detailed response possible.]

items.label: Analogy
items.prompt: [Explain using analogies and metaphors to make concepts relatable.]

items.label: Example
items.prompt: [Explain using lots of different concrete,most common, easy to understand examples make concepts relatable.]

items.label: Socratic
items.prompt: [Answer only with thought-provoking questions to guide understanding.]

items.label: Dialog
items.helper: Present the answer as a dialog.
items.prompt: [Present the explanation as a dialog between characters.]

items.label: Inversion
items.prompt: [Explain by describing what NOT to do and common mistakes to avoid.]

items.label: Manifesto
items.prompt: [Explain with the passion and conviction of a manifesto.]

items.label: Rant
items.prompt: [Give an entertaining rant-style explanation with strong opinions.]

items.label: Interactive Simulation
items.prompt: [Create an interactive HTML simulation to demonstrate the concept. Include complete HTML/CSS/JS code that can run standalone.]

items.label: Table of Contents
items.helper: Answer like a textbook table of contents.
items.prompt: [Explain as table of content of a Textbook]

items.label: 80/20 Rule
items.helper: Focus on the critical few drivers.
items.prompt: [Apply the 80/20 Pareto Principle: identify the critical 20% of inputs, causes, or actions that drive 80% of the results. Cut through the noise and ruthlessly focus on what actually moves the needle.]

items.label: 2nd Order Thinking
items.prompt: [Use second-order thinking: go beyond the immediate first consequence. For every effect, ask 'and then what?' Reveal the downstream chain reactions, unintended outcomes, and long-term implications that most people miss.]

items.label: Tier List
items.prompt: [Rank and categorize everything into a tier list using S (exceptional/elite), A (excellent), B (good), C (average), D (below average), and F (failing/avoid). Be decisive, opinionated, and justify each placement clearly.]

items.label: First Principles
items.helper: Strip to fundamentals and reason upward.
items.prompt: [Use first-principles thinking: strip away all assumptions, conventions, and analogies. Decompose the problem down to its most fundamental, irreducible truths. Then reason upward from those foundations to construct a fresh, clear answer from the ground up.]

items.label: Ayn Rand
items.prompt: [Answer in the voice, tone, and philosophical style of Ayn Rand. Adopt their worldview, vocabulary, and rhetorical patterns. Stay in character throughout.]
